case_laws.title: FIA v. Muhammad Hassan
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding online harassment and cyberstalking under PECA 2016. The court held that sending threatening messages, creating fake social media profiles, and sharing private information online constitutes offenses under Sections 21 and 24 of PECA.
case_laws.summary_ur: پیکا 2016 کے تحت آن لائن ہراسانی اور سائبر اسٹاکنگ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Cybercrime - PECA 2016 - Online harassment - Cyberstalking - Digital evidence - Section 21 PECA

case_laws.title: FIA Cybercrime Wing v. Sana Malik
case_laws.summary_en: Case on unauthorized access to computer systems under PECA Section 3. The court held that accessing another person's social media accounts without authorization constitutes an offense. The court emphasized that intent must be established and accidental access is not punishable.
case_laws.summary_ur: پیکا دفعہ 3 کے تحت کمپیوٹر سسٹمز تک غیر مجاز رسائی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Unauthorized access - PECA Section 3 - Social media accounts - Intent required - Accidental access excluded

case_laws.title: FIA v. Online Fraud Network
case_laws.summary_en: Case on electronic fraud under PECA Section 13. The court convicted the accused for phishing attacks and online banking fraud. The court held that creating fake websites to obtain banking credentials constitutes electronic fraud punishable with up to three years imprisonment.
case_laws.summary_ur: پیکا دفعہ 13 کے تحت الیکٹرانک فراڈ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Electronic fraud - PECA Section 13 - Phishing - Online banking fraud - Fake websites - Three years imprisonment

case_laws.title: The State v. Cyber Defamation Case
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the constitutionality of Section 20 PECA relating to offenses against modesty. The court struck down portions of Section 20 as being overbroad and violating freedom of expression under Article 19 of the Constitution. The court directed Parliament to narrowly define the offense.
case_laws.summary_ur: دفعہ 20 پیکا کی آئینی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: PECA Section 20 - Constitutionality - Overbroad provision - Freedom of expression - Article 19 - Struck down
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act", "Constitution of Pakistan 1973"]

case_laws.title: FIA v. Data Breach Suspects
case_laws.summary_en: Case on data breaches and unauthorized copying of data under PECA Section 6. The court held that copying or transmitting data from a computer system without authorization is punishable. Organizations holding personal data have a duty to implement reasonable security measures.
case_laws.summary_ur: ڈیٹا کی خلاف ورزی اور غیر مجاز نقل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Data breach - PECA Section 6 - Unauthorized copying - Duty to secure data - Reasonable security measures

case_laws.title: Online Harassment Victim v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on non-consensual sharing of intimate images under PECA. The court held that sharing or threatening to share intimate images without consent constitutes an offense under Sections 20 and 21 PECA. Victims are entitled to protection orders and the removal of such content.
case_laws.summary_ur: غیر رضامندی سے ذاتی تصاویر کی شیئرنگ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Non-consensual intimate images - PECA Sections 20, 21 - Protection orders - Content removal - Victim rights

case_laws.title: FIA v. Hate Speech Online
case_laws.summary_en: Case on hate speech and incitement to violence through social media under PECA Section 11-W. The court discussed the balance between free speech and prevention of hate speech online, holding that speech inciting violence against identifiable groups is not protected under Article 19.
case_laws.summary_ur: سوشل میڈیا کے ذریعے نفرت انگیز تقریر سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Hate speech online - PECA - Incitement to violence - Free speech limits - Article 19 - Identifiable groups
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act", "Constitution of Pakistan 1973"]

case_laws.title: National Bank of Pakistan v. Muhammad Tariq
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding recovery of bank loans and jurisdiction of Banking Courts. The court held that Banking Courts have exclusive jurisdiction over recovery of finance under FIO 2001. The limitation period for filing recovery suits was also clarified.
case_laws.summary_ur: بینک قرضوں کی وصولی اور بینکنگ کورٹس کے دائرہ اختیار سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Banking Court jurisdiction - Recovery of finance - Limitation period - FIO 2001

case_laws.title: Allied Bank v. Consumer Protection Court
case_laws.summary_en: Case on banking fraud and liability of banks for unauthorized transactions. The court held that a bank has a duty to verify the identity of the account holder before processing transactions. Unauthorized ATM withdrawals due to bank's negligence in maintaining security make the bank liable.
case_laws.summary_ur: بینکنگ فراڈ اور غیر مجاز لین دین میں بینک کی ذمہ داری سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Banking fraud - Unauthorized transactions - Bank's duty to verify - ATM security - Bank's negligence - Liability

case_laws.title: Meezan Bank v. Defaulting Borrower
case_laws.summary_en: Case on Islamic banking and Murabaha financing disputes. The court held that Murabaha contracts must comply with Shariah principles and the difference between the cost price and selling price is the bank's profit, not interest. Default in Murabaha payments is enforceable through Banking Courts.
case_laws.summary_ur: اسلامی بینکنگ اور مرابحہ فنانسنگ کے تنازعات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Islamic banking - Murabaha - Shariah compliance - Profit not interest - Banking Court enforcement

case_laws.title: State Bank v. Micro Finance Banks Association
case_laws.summary_en: Case on SBP's regulatory authority over microfinance banks. The court upheld SBP's power to issue prudential regulations, set capital adequacy requirements, and impose penalties on non-compliant microfinance institutions. SBP's regulations have the force of law.
case_laws.summary_ur: مائیکروفنانس بینکوں پر ایس بی پی کے ریگولیٹری اختیار سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: SBP regulatory authority - Microfinance banks - Prudential regulations - Capital adequacy - Penalties

case_laws.title: Askari Bank v. Guarantor Liability Case
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the liability of guarantors in banking transactions. The court held that a guarantor's liability is co-extensive with the principal debtor. The bank can proceed directly against the guarantor without first exhausting remedies against the principal debtor.
case_laws.summary_ur: بینکنگ لین دین میں ضامنوں کی ذمہ داری سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Guarantor liability - Co-extensive with debtor - Direct action against guarantor - No exhaustion requirement

case_laws.title: UBL v. Letter of Credit Dispute
case_laws.summary_en: Case on letter of credit disputes in international trade. The court applied the principle of strict compliance, holding that the bank is obligated to pay on a letter of credit only if the documents presented strictly comply with the terms and conditions of the LC.
case_laws.summary_ur: بین الاقوامی تجارت میں لیٹر آف کریڈٹ کے تنازعات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Letter of credit - Strict compliance - Document examination - Bank's obligation to pay - International trade

case_laws.title: Federation of Pakistan v. Mst. Kaneez Fatima
case_laws.judge_name: Justice Allama Dr. Fida Muhammad Khan
case_laws.summary_en: Case examining the Islamic inheritance rights of female heirs. The Federal Shariat Court affirmed that denying inheritance shares to women as prescribed in the Holy Quran is against Islamic injunctions. The court directed enforcement of women's inheritance rights.
case_laws.summary_ur: خواتین وارثین کے اسلامی وراثت کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Islamic inheritance - Women's share - Wirasat - Quranic injunctions - Enforcement
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act", "Prevention of Anti-Women Practices Act"]

case_laws.title: Islamic Banking Review Case
case_laws.summary_en: The Federal Shariat Court examined the compliance of contemporary banking practices with Islamic principles. The court directed that interest (Riba) in all its forms is prohibited under Islamic law and financial institutions must transition to Shariah-compliant modes of financing.
case_laws.summary_ur: فیڈرل شریعت کورٹ نے جدید بینکنگ طریقوں کی اسلامی اصولوں سے مطابقت کا جائزہ لیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Islamic banking - Riba prohibition - Interest-free banking - Shariah compliance - Transition timeline

case_laws.title: Waqf Board v. Illegal Occupants
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the protection of Waqf properties. The court held that Waqf properties are inalienable and cannot be sold, mortgaged, or transferred. Illegal occupation of Waqf land can be challenged through Waqf tribunal and the occupants must be evicted.
case_laws.summary_ur: وقف جائیدادوں کے تحفظ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Waqf property - Inalienable - Cannot be sold or mortgaged - Illegal occupation - Eviction - Waqf tribunal

case_laws.title: Zakat Collection Dispute
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the compulsory deduction of Zakat from bank accounts. The court upheld the Zakat and Ushr Ordinance 1980 but held that individuals who submit a declaration of being from a school of thought that does not permit compulsory deduction are exempt.
case_laws.summary_ur: بینک اکاؤنٹس سے زکوٰۃ کی لازمی کٹوتی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Zakat - Compulsory deduction - Bank accounts - Exemption declaration - School of thought - Zakat Ordinance 1980

case_laws.title: Shehla Zia v. WAPDA
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the right to privacy and environmental rights. The court held that the right to life under Article 9 includes the right to live in a safe environment free from health hazards. Installation of high-voltage power lines near residential areas requires environmental impact assessment.
case_laws.summary_ur: رازداری کے حق اور ماحولیاتی حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Right to privacy - Environmental rights - Article 9 - Health hazards - Environmental impact assessment

case_laws.title: Transgender Rights Association v. Government of Punjab
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark case on transgender rights in Pakistan. The court recognized the fundamental rights of transgender persons under Articles 9, 14, and 25 of the Constitution. The court directed government agencies to issue CNIC cards with gender identity and ensure non-discrimination in employment and education.
case_laws.summary_ur: پاکستان میں خواجہ سرا حقوق سے متعلق تاریخی مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Transgender rights - CNIC gender identity - Non-discrimination - Articles 9, 14, 25 - Employment and education

case_laws.title: Child Labour Elimination Case
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court suo motu case on child labor. The court held that employing children below 14 years in hazardous occupations violates Article 11(3) of the Constitution. The court directed enforcement agencies to take action against employers using child labor and ordered rehabilitation of rescued children.
case_laws.summary_ur: چائلڈ لیبر سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا ازخود نوٹس مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Child labor - Article 11(3) Constitution - Hazardous occupations - Below 14 years - Enforcement - Rehabilitation

case_laws.title: Women Rights Organization v. Government of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: Case on sexual harassment at workplace under the Protection against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act 2010. The court held that every organization must constitute an inquiry committee and adopt a code of conduct. Failure to do so makes the employer liable.
case_laws.summary_ur: کام کی جگہ پر جنسی ہراسانی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Sexual harassment - Workplace - Inquiry committee - Code of conduct - Employer liability - Act of 2010

case_laws.title: Forced Conversion Case
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on forced conversion of religious minorities. The court held that forced conversion violates Article 20 (freedom of religion) of the Constitution. The court directed that all allegations of forced conversion must be thoroughly investigated and the victim must be produced before a judicial magistrate.
case_laws.summary_ur: مذہبی اقلیتوں کی جبری تبدیلی مذہب سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Forced conversion - Freedom of religion - Article 20 - Minorities' rights - Investigation - Judicial magistrate

case_laws.title: Missing Persons Commission Case
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court addressed enforced disappearances and the right to life. The court held that enforced disappearance violates Articles 9, 10, and 14 of the Constitution. Security agencies must produce missing persons before courts and cannot detain individuals without legal authority.
case_laws.summary_ur: جبری گمشدگی اور حق زندگی سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Enforced disappearances - Right to life - Articles 9, 10, 14 - Security agencies - Production before court

case_laws.title: Environmental Protection Tribunal v. Industrial Polluters
case_laws.summary_en: Case on industrial pollution and the powers of Environmental Tribunals under PEPA 1997. The court upheld heavy fines imposed on factories discharging untreated industrial waste into rivers. The court held that the polluter pays principle applies and industries must install effluent treatment plants.
case_laws.summary_ur: صنعتی آلودگی اور ماحولیاتی ٹربیونلز کے اختیارات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Industrial pollution - Environmental Tribunal - PEPA 1997 - Polluter pays principle - Effluent treatment

case_laws.title: Climate Change Case
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark Supreme Court case on climate change and government obligations. The court held that the government has a constitutional obligation under Articles 9 and 14 to protect citizens from the adverse effects of climate change. The court directed creation of a Climate Change Commission.
case_laws.summary_ur: موسمیاتی تبدیلی اور حکومتی ذمہ داریوں سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا تاریخی مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Climate change - Government obligations - Articles 9, 14 - Climate Change Commission - Constitutional duty

case_laws.title: Clean Air Petition v. EPA Punjab
case_laws.summary_en: Case on air pollution and the obligation of EPA to enforce air quality standards. The court directed EPA Punjab to monitor and enforce National Environmental Quality Standards (NEQS). Burning of crop residue and industrial emissions exceeding NEQS are illegal.
case_laws.summary_ur: فضائی آلودگی اور ای پی اے کی ہوا کے معیار نافذ کرنے کی ذمہ داری سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Air pollution - EPA enforcement - NEQS standards - Crop burning - Industrial emissions

case_laws.title: Forest Conservation Case
case_laws.summary_en: Case on illegal deforestation and encroachment on protected forest land. The court held that forests are a national asset protected under environmental law and the public trust doctrine. Illegal timber logging and encroachment on forest land must be prosecuted and forest area restored.
case_laws.summary_ur: غیر قانونی جنگلات کی کٹائی اور محفوظ جنگلاتی زمین پر تجاوزات سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Deforestation - Protected forest land - Public trust doctrine - Illegal logging - Forest restoration

case_laws.title: Software Company v. Copyright Infringer
case_laws.summary_en: Case on copyright infringement of computer software. The court held that unauthorized copying and distribution of copyrighted software constitutes infringement under the Copyright Ordinance 1962. The court granted injunction and damages in favor of the copyright holder.
case_laws.summary_ur: کمپیوٹر سافٹ ویئر کی کاپی رائٹ خلاف ورزی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Copyright infringement - Computer software - Unauthorized copying - Copyright Ordinance 1962 - Injunction and damages

case_laws.title: Pharmaceutical Co. v. Generic Medicine Manufacturer
case_laws.summary_en: Case on patent rights and compulsory licensing in pharmaceutical industry. The court discussed the balance between patent protection and public health. The court upheld the government's power to grant compulsory licenses for patented medicines in the interest of public health.
case_laws.summary_ur: دواسازی کی صنعت میں پیٹنٹ کے حقوق اور لازمی لائسنسنگ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Patent rights - Compulsory licensing - Pharmaceuticals - Public health - Patents Ordinance 2000

case_laws.title: Brand Owner v. Counterfeit Goods Importer
case_laws.summary_en: Case on trademark infringement and passing off. The court held that importing and selling counterfeit goods bearing registered trademarks constitutes infringement. The court granted Anton Piller order for search and seizure of counterfeit goods and awarded damages.
case_laws.summary_ur: ٹریڈ مارک کی خلاف ورزی اور نقلی سامان سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Trademark infringement - Passing off - Counterfeit goods - Anton Piller order - Search and seizure - Damages

case_laws.title: Music Label v. Online Piracy Platform
case_laws.summary_en: Case on online piracy and copyright infringement through digital platforms. The court ordered blocking of websites hosting pirated content and held that internet service providers must comply with court orders to block access to infringing websites.
case_laws.summary_ur: ڈیجیٹل پلیٹ فارمز کے ذریعے آن لائن پائریسی اور کاپی رائٹ خلاف ورزی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Online piracy - Copyright infringement - Website blocking - ISP compliance - Digital platforms
